title: Implement container restarting
body: Wire in logic for restarting containers

comments.body: This probably needs some tests, I'll add those after lunch
@mheon good job on getting this to work.

Bad news: this has caused the old attach bug (eating output if you use a one-shot command that immediately exits) to resurface in `podman start --attach`. We can't use our old fix because we can no longer guarantee that the attach socket is present and valid before restart.

This might be a conmon bug.
The reorder of operations in attach.go is unworkable. We probably need conmon changes to reopen the unix socket if it already exists.

I've added a test and undone the reorder in `start.go`. As a result, `podman start --attach` will be broken when restarting containers, but will still work normally otherwise. `podman start` and then `podman attach` should work fine.
I'm going to mark this WIP. I'd like to add a few more tests, and I'm convinced the API needs work.
I've solved our attach issues. Now just needs an extra test or two
Found a bug in the 'podman start' unit tests my rewrite of attach code exposed. Fixed it.
Removing WIP label, should be ready for review/merge
LGTM, he addressed the comment i had

:pushpin: Commit dbf900e has been approved by `baude`
